steps.title: Spot the opposition between sentences
steps.content: The first sentence says the volcanoes "may look similar from afar." The second reveals experts "can tell" they are different types. This contradiction—appearance vs. reality—demands a contrast transition word.

steps.title: Choose the contrast word
steps.content:
The logical relationship is contrast — the volcanoes look similar from afar, yet experts can tell them apart. The blank needs a transition signaling that opposition.
  • Choice A — "therefore,": signals cause-effect (X, so Y), so fails — the second sentence contradicts the first, it does not follow from it. ✗
  • Choice B — "for example,": signals a specific instance of a general claim, so fails — no example is being given. ✗
  • Choice C — "though,": signals contrast (despite appearances, the reality is different), so fits. ✓
  • Choice D — "in addition,": signals addition of a new, compatible point, so fails — the second sentence contradicts, not adds to, the first. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
